name: ENRICH
fullName: Early Minimally Invasive Removal of Intracerebral Hemorrhage
population: n=300, 18-80, NIHSS n/a, ≤24 h from onset
teachingPoint: Supports early minimally invasive evacuation for lobar ICH at capable centers.

name: MISTIE III
fullName: Minimally Invasive Surgery with Thrombolysis in ICH Evacuation
teachingPoint: No overall functional benefit; safe; outcome depends on achieving adequate evacuation.

name: MIND
fullName: Minimally Invasive Surgery vs Medical Management Alone for Intracerebral Hemorrhage: The MIND Randomized Clinical Trial
teachingPoint: Tempers enthusiasm for minimally invasive ICH evacuation; MIS benefit appears pathway- and technique-specific (cf. ENRICH), not universal.

name: SWITCH
fullName: Decompressive Craniectomy plus Best Medical Treatment vs Best Medical Treatment Alone for Severe Deep ICH
population: n=201, 18-75, NIHSS n/a, Severe deep (basal ganglia/thalamic) spontaneous ICH
finding: Favoured surgery numerically but not significant: 44% vs 58%
teachingPoint: Objective: numerically fewer very-poor outcomes with craniectomy, but not statistically significant; no safety excess.
